title: 中考六选五 13: Repairing Instead of Replacing
passage:
When something breaks, many people choose to buy a new one. A phone, a bag or a pair of shoes may be thrown away as soon as it stops looking perfect. 1. ______ However, repairing things can still be a wise choice.

Repairing can save money. Sometimes a small problem only needs a simple fix. 2. ______ Buying a new product may cost much more than changing one part or sewing one line.

Repairing is also better for the environment. Making new products requires materials, energy and transport. 3. ______ If people repair and use things longer, they can reduce waste and pollution.

There is another hidden value: repairing teaches patience and responsibility. When students learn to fix something, they understand that objects are not meaningless. 4. ______ This attitude can also influence how they treat resources in general.

Of course, not everything should be repaired. If something is unsafe or the repair costs too much, replacing it may be reasonable. 5. ______ The key is to think before throwing things away.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: A broken zipper or loose button does not always mean the whole thing is useless.
But replacement should not become the first and only habit.
This creates an environmental cost that people often ignore.
This habit is common in a society that values speed and convenience.
They begin to see the effort and resources behind everyday things.
Repairing things is always cheaper than buying new ones.
